summary: In a brief 1932 address on his 93rd birthday, John D. Rockefeller offers a message of optimism during the Great Depression. Drawing on his long life experience, he assures the public that economic depressions are cyclical and prosperity will return, urging a recommitment to foundational values like liberty, unselfishness, and faith.

principles.statement: Prosperity will always return after a depression.
principles.rationale: Based on nearly a century of lived experience, economic downturns are cyclical and temporary rather than permanent.
principles.source_quote: in the 93 years of my life depressions have come and gone Prosperity has always returned and will again

principles.statement: Society must be grounded in liberty, unselfish devotion to the common good, and belief in God.
principles.rationale: These are the fundamental principles upon which the country was founded and are necessary to courageously build a better world.
principles.source_quote: I desire to reaffirm my belief in the fundamental principles upon which this country was founded Liberty unselfish Devotion to the common good and belief in God

mental_models.name: Cyclical History
mental_models.description: Viewing economic and societal hardships not as permanent states, but as temporary phases in a continuous cycle where recovery is inevitable.
mental_models.example: Using his 93 years of life experience to contextualize the Great Depression as just another downturn that has 'come and gone' before.

signature_quotes.text: in the 93 years of my life depressions have come and gone Prosperity has always returned and will again
signature_quotes.context: Spoken during the Great Depression to offer historical perspective and hope.

signature_quotes.text: let us with faith in God in ourselves and in humanity go forward courage courageously resolve to play our part worthily in building a better world
signature_quotes.context: Concluding call to action in his 93rd birthday address.

anti_patterns.why_it_fails: These are historical mistakes that undermine the common good and must be recognized with humility in order to move forward.
